meta_title: For Freelancers: API integration guide to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: API Integration Guide: Debugging Code for Freelancers
content.seo_description: Master API integration to crush debugging bottlenecks. A high-impact guide for freelancers using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster and impress clients.
content.answer_block: Debugging API failures requires a systematic decoupling of the request payload from the service response. By using Claude Code to trace execution and Antigravity to instrument mock endpoints, you can isolate logic errors in minutes rather than hours, ensuring your client projects remain on schedule and error-free.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and your terminal is bleeding red error codes. You've spent hours tracing an API integration that refuses to handshake, leaving you trapped in a cycle of trial-and-error that kills your profitability and your sanity. We’ve all been there—staring at a 500 Internal Server Error, wondering where the payload went wrong.

But professional engineering isn't about brute-forcing fixes; it's about building a robust debugging harness. By combining the analytical depth of Claude Code with the rapid prototyping power of Antigravity, you can transform your debugging workflow from a reactive nightmare into a surgical, highly repeatable process that keeps your code clean and your clients happy.

steps.title: Establish an API Observability Layer
steps.description: Before fixing, you must see the data flow. Use Antigravity to generate a middleware layer that logs every request and response header for easier analysis.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an Express middleware that logs full request/response payloads to a local JSON file, filtering out sensitive authorization tokens.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply logging middleware to api/routes.ts'

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a binary search on your integration logic to determine if the error lies in the request construction or the response parsing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this API client code and generate a test suite that mocks the downstream response to isolate the parser logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'identify the exact line where the API response fails validation'

steps.title: Simulate Edge Cases with Antigravity
steps.description: Don't just test the success path. Use Antigravity to mock failure states like 429 Rate Limits or 503 Service Unavailable to ensure your error handling is production-ready.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate five mock responses for the external API, including success, 400 bad request, 401 unauthorized, 429 rate limit, and 500 server error.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement robust error handling for the five mocked API states'

steps.title: Automate Schema Validation
steps.description: Eliminate 'undefined' errors by forcing your integration to validate against an OpenAPI/Swagger schema automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Zod schema validator based on this API documentation to enforce strict typing on all incoming response objects.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'replace manual response parsing with Zod schema validation'

steps.title: Refactor for Resiliency
steps.description: Wrap your API calls in a retry mechanism with exponential backoff to handle transient network issues, a must for high-quality freelance deliverables.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the current fetch implementation to include a retry wrapper with exponential backoff for network-related failures.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply exponential backoff pattern to all external API calls'

pro_tips: Always log the 'x-request-id' header from the provider; it is the single most valuable piece of data for support tickets.
Use a local proxy like 'mitmproxy' if Claude Code cannot access the live API endpoint due to local firewall restrictions.
Never hardcode environment variables; use a .env.example template to ensure your production config is never accidentally exposed.
Implement circuit breakers for third-party APIs to prevent your entire application from hanging when a provider goes down.
Treat your API integration tests as documentation; keep them in a dedicated /tests/api folder so clients can see your testing rigor.
content.next_steps: Once your API integration is stable, move to implementing automated CI/CD pipelines to ensure future code changes don't break your validated endpoints.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start shipping features that scale. Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Use Claude Code to perform a binary search on your integration logic to determine if the error lies in the request construction or the response parsing.
Don't just test the success path. Use Antigravity to mock failure states like 429 Rate Limits or 503 Service Unavailable to ensure your error handling is production-ready.
Eliminate 'undefined' errors by forcing your integration to validate against an OpenAPI/Swagger schema automatically.
Wrap your API calls in a retry mechanism with exponential backoff to handle transient network issues, a must for high-quality freelance deliverables.
